persons.arguments_against: The counter-case would be a same-name relative occupying one of these governorships. It collapses against an exceptionally distinctive three-forename identity (Frederick John Dealtry), only ONE bio with this surname, and a unique honour set (G.C.M.G./K.C.M.G., C.B., D.S.O.) that the stints carry. Northern Nigeria 1905: bio 'high commnstr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899; resigned Sept., 1906' matches 'High Commissioner' 1905-06 in Northern Nigeria with C.B./D.S.O. -- and the bio's K.C.M.G. (1901) appears exactly when the stint upgrades C.M.G.->K.C.M.G. in 1906. Hong Kong 1911: bio 'gov. of Hong Kong, May, 1907' matches Governor of Hong Kong, with the stint printing the full name 'Frederick John Dealty'. Nigeria 1917: bio 'gov.-gen., Nigeria, Dec., 1913' matches 'Governor-General' of Nigeria 1917-18 with G.C.M.G./C.B./D.S.O. The tenure-overlap FAILs for the two Nigeria stints are mechanical (placed-event tenures not coded as spanning those exact years), but the named-office + place + honour + full-name agreement is overwhelming and there is no second F. J. D. Lugard in the record.

evidence_cited.fact: Sir F. J. D. Lugard | Governor-General
Sir F. J. D. Lugard | High Commissioner
high commnstr. for Northern Nigeria, Dec., 1899
Sir F. J. D. Lugard | Governor

persons.would_overturn: Evidence of a second person named Frederick John Dealtry Lugard simultaneously holding colonial office -- which would contradict the unique forename triple and the single biography for the surname.

notes: Lord Lugard (F. J. D. Lugard), the famous colonial administrator. Distinctive three-forename name, single bio for the surname, and offices/places/honours map directly to named bio clauses. The tenure-overlap FAILs are mechanical coding artifacts, not evidence against.
